resources.string: First-pass port from SwiftUI with USB discovery and watch-only controls.
Choose a connected USB capture device
No device found. Connect a Capture Card to continue.
You\'ll be asked to allow Camera, Mic and USB access, which is required because Android treats Capture Cards as USB Webcams.
no resolutions reported for this device
Low FPS detected. Tap for info.
The actual frame rate is lower than requested. This can be caused by USB bandwidth limitations, high system load, or capture card limitations. Try a lower resolution or a different USB port.
This capture card has known compatibility issues. Performance may be below the advertised resolution or frame rate settings.
Connect a USB capture device, select it from the list, and press Play.
For best results, select a frame rate that is equal to or higher than the frame rate of the source input. If playback frame rate is lower than expected, avoid USB hubs and replace low-quality cables.
Use the settings menu to rotate the picture, mirror the image, and show frame rate stats.
Use the playback controls to mute playback and set the volume.

While any USB Video Class (UVC) device should work with Consolation, video quality ultimately depends on the capture device hardware. Some devices may advertise resolutions and frame rates beyond their actual capabilities.
Consolation and the Consolation logo are trademarks of Centennial OSS Inc.\nAll rights reserved.
Consolation is a USB Capture Card utility for viewing gaming consoles, Raspberry Pis, and other HDMI devices on Android tablets.
External USB Video Class (UVC) capture hardware is required.
Consolation is 100% private. It does not collect analytics or snoop on your usage. Nothing ever leaves your device. Period.
This software is completely free and open source for you to enjoy.
USB permission is required before starting preview.
Camera permission is required before Android will show the USB access prompt for video-class (UVC) devices. Grant it, then request USB permission again.
Microphone permission was denied; video will play without HDMI/capture-card audio from USB.
Camera and microphone access are required for USB video and for playing capture-card audio through the device speakers.
USB permission denied for the selected device.
Could not start USB permission request.
USB device is not connected or no longer matches the selected entry.
Could not start UVC preview on this USB path. Try a direct port (no hub), a different cable or hub, or unplug and replug the capture device.

Android could not expose an audio input for the selected capture card. This can happen when multiple USB capture or USB audio devices are connected at the same time. Disconnect the extra USB devices, then stop and start playback again.
USB diagnostics written to Logcat (tag ConsolationUsbDiag). Filter: adb logcat -s ConsolationUsbDiag:I
